# terrible_cisco_dnac_path_trace_workflow_manager (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config` (String) A list containing the details for Path Trace configuration.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `config_verify` (Boolean) Set to `true` to enable configuration verification on Cisco DNA Center after applying
the playbook configuration. This ensures that the system validates the configuration
state after the change is applied.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Specifies the desired state for the configuration. If `merged`, the module will create
or update the configuration, adding new settings or modifying existing ones. If `deleted`,
it will remove the specified settings.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `response_1` (String) A dictionary with the response returned by the Cisco Catalyst Center Python SDK
- `response_2` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco Catalyst Center Python SDK
- `response_3` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco Catalyst Center Python SDK
- `response_4` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco Catalyst Center Python SDK
- `response_5` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco Catalyst Center Python SDK
